title: smilax microphylla subsp. elongata
intro: Smilax microphylla subsp. elongata is a plant species known as a type of greenbrier or cat's claw vine, belonging to the Smilacaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Smilax microphylla subsp. elongata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various digestive issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to support immune function and overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
